title: ACDP Transparency-Log Inclusion Proof (0.3.0)
description: An inclusion proof that one leaf is committed by a checkpointed transparency-log tree (RFC-ACDP-0012 §8.2, §9.1): the RFC 6962 §2.1.1 audit path for leaf_index at tree_size, together with the signed checkpoint it verifies against. Returned by GET /log/proof (inclusion mode) and OPTIONALLY carried as the top-level 'log_inclusion' member of the full-retrieval envelope (RFC-ACDP-0012 §10 — a sibling of registry_receipt, never a member of it). Specified by RFC-ACDP-0012 (acdp/0.3.0), promoting the RFC-ACDP-0009 §2.11 reservation; additive minor, so this schema lives in the v0.1.0 namespace (VERSIONING.md).

log_id.description: The log instantiation this proof belongs to. MUST equal log_checkpoint.log_id (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.1 step 4).

leaf_index.description: The 0-based append-order position of the leaf. MUST be < tree_size.

tree_size.description: The tree size the proof is computed against. MUST equal log_checkpoint.tree_size (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.1 step 4).

inclusion_path.description: The RFC 6962 §2.1.1 audit path PATH(leaf_index, D[tree_size]), ordered from the lowest tree level upward; each element is 'sha256:' + lowercase hex of a raw 32-byte node digest. May be empty (tree_size 1). Verified by the RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.1 folding algorithm against the checkpoint's root_hash.

log_checkpoint.description: The signed checkpoint (RFC-ACDP-0012 §6) this proof verifies against, verified per RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3.

leaf.description: OPTIONAL convenience echo of the leaf object, present on GET /log/proof inclusion-mode responses only when the requester is authorized to retrieve the context (RFC-ACDP-0012 §8.2), and absent from the retrieval-envelope log_inclusion member (§10). Verifiers MUST NOT trust it — the leaf is independently reconstructed from verified body and receipt material (§9.1 step 1).

$comment: Closed schema (RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.1 openness posture for proof objects): a proof is arithmetic evidence with no extension surface; unknown members would be unverifiable. Consistency-mode GET /log/proof responses use a distinct shape (first_tree_size / second_tree_size / consistency_path — RFC-ACDP-0012 §8.2) and are not instances of this schema.
